name: Principles of Machine Learning
whatYouLearn: Core ML algorithms: decision trees, SVMs, kernel methods, Bayesian learning, ensemble methods, and data mining techniques.
contents: Supervised and unsupervised learning, statistical learning theory, optimization, regularization, model selection, evaluation methodology.
professorResearch: Director of Fraunhofer IAIS and Bonn Director of Lamarr Institute. Research in ML, trustworthy AI, data mining.
hiwiPotential: High – Fraunhofer IAIS offers many HiWi/research positions in applied ML.
thesisPotential: Very High – Gateway to Lamarr Institute and Fraunhofer IAIS thesis topics.
strategicAdvice: MUST TAKE in Semester 1. Foundation course that unlocks the entire ML track. Absolutely critical for PhD preparation.

name: Introduction to Natural Language Processing
whatYouLearn: Foundations of NLP: text classification, language models, sequence-to-sequence, attention mechanisms, transformers, and LLMs.
contents: Text preprocessing, word embeddings, RNNs, attention, transformers, pre-trained language models, sentiment analysis, NER, machine translation.

recommended: Python, basic ML knowledge, linear algebra

professorResearch: ERC Starting Grant (€1.5M) awardee. Research in conversational AI, user modeling, AI robustness, bias in LLMs, personalization. Lamarr Institute member.
hiwiPotential: Very High – CAISA Lab actively hires HiWis for NLP/LLM projects.
thesisPotential: Very High – Cutting-edge LLM/NLP thesis topics. Direct path to top NLP venues (ACL, EMNLP).
strategicAdvice: MUST TAKE in Semester 1. Absolutely critical for your NLP research trajectory. Required for the NLP Lab.

name: Cognitive Robotics
whatYouLearn: Robot perception, sensor processing, SLAM, motion planning, autonomous navigation, and manipulation.
contents: Probabilistic robotics, sensor models, Kalman/particle filters, SLAM, path planning, manipulation, ROS.
professorResearch: ELLIS Fellow, Lamarr Institute PI for Embodied AI, NimbRo team lead (RoboCup champion), ICRA 2027 Program Co-Chair.
hiwiPotential: Very High – NimbRo team constantly needs members for robot competitions.
thesisPotential: Very High – World-class robotics lab with hardware access.
strategicAdvice: Take in Semester 1 if interested in robotics. Major prerequisite for all advanced robotics courses and labs.

name: Robot Learning
whatYouLearn: Deep learning for robotics, imitation learning, reinforcement learning for robot control, sim-to-real transfer.
contents: Deep learning for visual perception, imitation learning, policy optimization, sim-to-real, manipulation learning.
professorResearch: ELLIS Fellow, leader of NimbRo. Research in deep learning for robot perception, manipulation, and autonomous systems.
hiwiPotential: Very High – Research-intensive course, direct bridge to NimbRo positions.
thesisPotential: Very High – Thesis topics at the intersection of ML and robotics.
strategicAdvice: Take in Semester 2 after Cognitive Robotics. This course directly connects ML and robotics for PhD-quality research.

name: Reinforcement Learning
whatYouLearn: Markov decision processes, value functions, Q-learning, deep Q-learning, policy gradient methods, Monte Carlo tree search.
contents: State space models, tree search, MCTS, MDP, Bellman equations, TD learning, Q-learning, deep Q-learning.

required: Linear algebra, statistics, probability theory, Python

professorResearch: Lead Scientist for ML at Fraunhofer IAIS, co-director of Lamarr Institute. Research in hybrid ML, quantum ML, pattern recognition.
hiwiPotential: High – Fraunhofer IAIS positions in applied ML/RL.
thesisPotential: High – Opportunity for RL-focused thesis at Fraunhofer IAIS.
strategicAdvice: Take when offered (every 2 years!). Critical RL foundations. Check summer/winter schedule carefully as it may not be offered every semester.

name: Lab Natural Language Processing
whatYouLearn: Hands-on NLP system development: LLMs, conversational systems, computational social science. Real research project with CAISA Lab advisor.
contents: NLP tutorials, LLM fine-tuning, project-based learning with individual CAISA Lab researcher supervision, final paper.
professorResearch: ERC Starting Grant, CAISA Lab director. Research in conversational AI, social NLP, AI alignment.
hiwiPotential: Extremely High – Lab is the gateway to a CAISA Lab HiWi position.
thesisPotential: Extremely High – Best path to an NLP thesis with Prof. Flek. Lab project can evolve into thesis.
strategicAdvice: Take in Semester 2 (after NLP intro). This is THE most important lab for your NLP trajectory. Apply early!

name: Seminar Large Language Models
whatYouLearn: Cutting-edge LLM research: hallucination, reasoning, safety, alignment, multilinguality, compression, LLM agents.
contents: Paper reading and presentation on: hallucination reduction, explainability, safety/bias, personalization, sustainability, multimodality, LLM agents.
professorResearch: ERC Starting Grant. Leading research on LLM safety, personalization, and social NLP.
hiwiPotential: High – Demonstrates research capability for CAISA positions.
thesisPotential: Very High – Direct pipeline to LLM thesis topics.
strategicAdvice: Take in Semester 2 alongside NLP Lab if possible. Max 35 participants – apply early!

name: Lab AI Alignment
whatYouLearn: AI alignment research: value alignment, emergent misalignment, scalable oversight, mechanistic interpretability.
contents: Lectures + programming exercises weeks 1-5, project idea presentation, midterm + final presentations, student paper.
professorResearch: ERC-funded research on AI trustworthiness, robustness, ethical AI, alignment.
hiwiPotential: Very High – Cutting-edge alignment research positions at CAISA.
thesisPotential: Very High – Hot research area with high publication potential.
strategicAdvice: Alternative to NLP Lab if you're more interested in AI safety and alignment. Take in Semester 2 or 3.

name: Seminar Selected Topics in Natural Language Processing
whatYouLearn: Scientific paper reading, critical analysis, research presentations, technical report writing in NLP.
contents: Weekly seminar on selected NLP research topics, guest speaker presentations, scientific writing practice.
professorResearch: ERC Starting Grant, specializing in conversational AI and social NLP.
hiwiPotential: Medium – Good for building relationship with CAISA Lab.
thesisPotential: High – Develops research skills critical for thesis work.
strategicAdvice: Take as your mandatory seminar. Max 20 participants – limited slots. Apply early.

name: Algorithms for Data Science
professor: Dr. Tamas Horvath, Dr. Florian Seiffarth
whatYouLearn: Algorithmic foundations of data science: clustering, graph algorithms, kernel methods, dimensionality reduction.
contents: Clustering algorithms, density estimation, spectral methods, kernel methods, dimensionality reduction, graph-based methods.
professorResearch: Research in learning theory, graph kernels, pattern mining.
strategicAdvice: Good complementary course if you want deeper algorithmic ML foundations. Not as critical as 4111 but synergizes well.

name: Foundations of Data Science
whatYouLearn: Mathematical foundations: high-dimensional geometry, SVD, clustering, PCA, random projections, ML theory.
contents: High-dimensional spaces, SVD, clustering algorithms, random projection, supervised/unsupervised ML theory.
professorResearch: ERC Starting Grant, Lamarr Institute member.
hiwiPotential: Medium – Builds mathematical foundations useful for CAISA Lab work.
thesisPotential: Medium – Foundational course rather than directly thesis-enabling.
strategicAdvice: Heavy 9 ECTS course. Good if you want solid mathematical ML foundations. Consider workload carefully.

name: Self-supervised Learning
whatYouLearn: Learning from unlabeled data: autoencoders, contrastive learning, pre-text tasks, distillation, pseudo-labeling.
contents: Nearest neighbors, clustering, autoencoders, generative models, masking, contrastive learning, cycle consistency.
professorResearch: PhD from ETH Zürich, Lamarr Institute PI. Research in open-set perception, continual learning, embodied AI.
hiwiPotential: High – New lab actively building research team.
thesisPotential: Very High – At the frontier of self-supervised learning for robotics. High publication potential.
strategicAdvice: Excellent for bridging vision/NLP/robotics. JProf Blum is building his team – great opportunity for early-stage PhD students.

name: Lab Perception and Learning for Robotics
whatYouLearn: Practical robot perception: localization, navigation, manipulation, deep learning deployment, sensor models.
contents: Robot localization, planning, navigation, manipulation, practical DL, sensor models, GPU cluster usage.
professorResearch: Open-vocabulary perception, continual learning, embodied AI at the Lamarr Institute.
hiwiPotential: Extremely High – Lab is the direct recruitment pipeline for JProf Blum's group.
thesisPotential: Extremely High – Perfect pathway if interested in vision + robotics thesis.
strategicAdvice: Excellent alternative to NLP Lab if your interests lean towards vision/robotics. New professor = more mentorship opportunities.

name: Computer Vision
whatYouLearn: Complete CV toolkit: filters, segmentation, features, stereo vision, optical flow, pose estimation, 3D reconstruction.
contents: Linear filters, edges, Hough transform, segmentation, graph cuts, MRFs, EM, optical flow, stereo, 3D pose estimation.
professorResearch: Lamarr Institute PI. HoMe project (generative AI for human motion), JUPITER supercomputer access. Publishes at CVPR, ICCV, NeurIPS.
hiwiPotential: High – Active CV group with funded projects.
thesisPotential: Very High – Strong publication record at top venues.
strategicAdvice: Take if vision/VLM interests you. Heavy 9 ECTS course. Essential for cross-disciplinary CV+NLP work (VLMs).

name: Advanced Computer Vision
whatYouLearn: Modern DL for CV: image classification, object detection, action recognition, pose estimation, image synthesis, VLMs.
contents: CNN architectures, object detection, tracking, action recognition, face analysis, image synthesis, vision-language models.
professorResearch: Generative AI, action anticipation, video understanding, JUPITER supercomputer allocation.
hiwiPotential: High – Access to cutting-edge vision projects.
thesisPotential: Very High – Perfect for VLM-focused thesis.
strategicAdvice: Take in Semester 2 or 3 if pursuing the vision track. Covers modern DL including VLMs – directly relevant to your VLM interests.

name: Humanoid Robotics
whatYouLearn: Humanoid robot techniques: perception, navigation, inverse kinematics, grasping, balance control, walking.
contents: Sensing, perception, environment representations, active perception, IK, motion planning, grasping, balance, walking.
professorResearch: Vice Rector for Digitalization, Lamarr Institute member, PhenoRob PI. Research in navigation, HRI, embodied AI with LLMs/VLMs.
hiwiPotential: High – HRL offers HiWi positions in robot navigation.
thesisPotential: High – Thesis topics in humanoid navigation and manipulation.
strategicAdvice: Take if robot navigation/manipulation interests you. Prof. Bennewitz is using LLMs/VLMs for robot tasking – synergy with NLP.

name: Technical Neural Nets
whatYouLearn: Classical neural network architectures: MLPs, RBF nets, Hopfield nets, SOMs, SVMs, recurrent networks, RL.
contents: MLP, backpropagation, RBF, Hopfield, Kohonen SOMs, ART, LVQ, recurrent nets, RL, Q-learning, SVMs, neural hardware.
professorResearch: Technical neural networks applied to robotics and control.
thesisPotential: Low – Better as a building block than a thesis path.
strategicAdvice: Useful for broad NN foundations, but if you already know deep learning well from undergrad, prioritize more advanced courses.

name: Artificial Life
whatYouLearn: Evolutionary algorithms, genetic programming, swarm intelligence, artificial immune systems, self-organization.
contents: Evolutionary computation, genetic algorithms, PSO, swarm intelligence, artificial immune systems, multi-agent systems.
professorResearch: Bio-inspired computing and evolutionary robotics.
thesisPotential: Low – Niche area, less aligned with your core interests.
strategicAdvice: Popular among students as 'easier' IS course. Can be used to fill ECTS, but not strategically critical for your PhD goals.

name: Advanced Methods of Information Retrieval
whatYouLearn: Advanced IR methods: data structures for retrieval, knowledge graphs, neural IR, evaluation techniques.
contents: Ranking methods, structured data retrieval, knowledge graphs, neural IR, evaluation techniques.
professorResearch: Lamarr Institute PI. Research in spatio-temporal analytics, knowledge graphs (WorldKG), semantic web. ISWC awards.
hiwiPotential: Medium – DSIS group offers positions in data science projects.
thesisPotential: High – Relevant for RAG systems, knowledge graphs – connects to your RAG background.
strategicAdvice: Highly relevant for your RAG background. Neural IR + knowledge graphs are hot topics. Good complementary course.

name: Spatio-Temporal Data Analytics
whatYouLearn: Spatio-temporal data structures, analysis methods, predictive modeling for geographic/temporal data.
contents: Data structures, representation methods, spatio-temporal analysis, ML-based analytics, evaluation techniques.
professorResearch: Knowledge graphs, smart cities, mobility, spatio-temporal AI.
thesisPotential: Medium – Useful for applied data science thesis.
strategicAdvice: Good if interested in spatial/temporal data analysis. Not critical for core NLP/ML path.

name: Explainable AI and Applications
whatYouLearn: Explainable AI methods, neural-symbolic AI, cognitive maps, qualitative spatial reasoning.
contents: Dual-system theories, XAI methods, neural-symbolic AI, rotating sphere embeddings, syllogistic reasoning.
professorResearch: Applied ML, text mining, knowledge distillation, XAI.
thesisPotential: Medium – XAI is growing field but lab is less established for PhD pipeline.
strategicAdvice: Interesting complementary course, no prerequisites. XAI knowledge useful for trustworthy AI research.

name: Lab Hybrid Learning and Applications
whatYouLearn: Self-selected research: hybrid ML, foundation models + downstream tasks, hands-on model building.
contents: Student-driven research project merging ML and DL. Data preprocessing, model building, evaluation, optimization.

recommended: Basic ML understanding, own project ideas

professorResearch: Text mining, applied ML, hybrid learning systems.
thesisPotential: Medium – Good for applied ML thesis.
strategicAdvice: Max 10 participants, must email with research idea before first meeting. Good if you have a specific ML project idea.

name: Mining Media Data II
whatYouLearn: Advanced data mining with LLMs, transformers for text analysis, knowledge distillation, media analytics.
contents: Predictive analytics, LLM architectures, transformer-based text analysis, knowledge distillation, recommendation systems.

recommended: Data science, ML, programming, linear algebra

examType: Written exam (120 min) or oral exam
professorResearch: Text mining, behavioral prediction, LLM applications.
strategicAdvice: Lightweight 4 ECTS lecture covering LLMs and transformers. Good supplement but not a primary course.

name: Geometric Deep Learning
whatYouLearn: Neural networks on geometric data: permutation invariance, differential geometry, neural fields, physics-informed NNs.
contents: Permutation invariance, differential geometry, curse of dimensionality, neural fields, physics-informed neural networks.
professorResearch: Geometry processing meets ML. Research in shape analysis, geometric deep learning, GNNs.
thesisPotential: High – Novel intersection of geometry and ML.
strategicAdvice: Good if interested in geometric/structural deep learning. Complementary to standard vision courses.

name: Computational Neuroscience: Cognition and Behaviour
whatYouLearn: Computational models of biological cognition: decision-making, memory, movement planning, spatial navigation.
contents: Decision-making with noisy information, information representation, memory formation, movement planning, spatial navigation.
professorResearch: Computational neuroscience, reinforcement learning theory, cognitive modeling.
strategicAdvice: Interesting if you want to understand biological intelligence. Not essential for your core ML/NLP path.

name: Lab Cognitive Robotics
whatYouLearn: Hands-on robotics: ROS, SLAM, 3D perception, object detection, mobile manipulation, human-robot interaction.
contents: ROS middleware, SLAM, 3D object detection, person tracking, action recognition, mobile manipulation.

recommended: MA-INF 4113 Cognitive Robotics OR MA-INF 4114 Robot Learning

professorResearch: ELLIS Fellow, NimbRo team, Lamarr Institute PI. World champion in RoboCup.
hiwiPotential: Extremely High – Direct pipeline to NimbRo team membership and competition participation.
thesisPotential: Extremely High – Leads directly to thesis in Prof. Behnke's lab.
strategicAdvice: Top-tier robotics lab. Take Cognitive Robotics first. If robotics interests you, this is the gateway.

name: Lab Development and Application of Data Mining and Learning Systems
whatYouLearn: Design, implement, and evaluate ML/data mining systems on real-world and synthetic datasets.
contents: Implementation and evaluation of specialized ML/DM algorithms, open-source ML libraries, systematic experiments.

recommended: MA-INF 4111 ML OR MA-INF 4112 Algorithms for Data Science

professorResearch: Director of Fraunhofer IAIS, Lamarr Institute director. Trustworthy AI, data mining.
hiwiPotential: Very High – Gateway to Fraunhofer IAIS positions.
thesisPotential: Very High – Direct thesis path with Prof. Wrobel and Fraunhofer.
strategicAdvice: Alternative lab path if you want industry-strength ML experience. Fraunhofer IAIS connection is valuable.

name: Biomedical Data Science and AI
whatYouLearn: Statistical techniques, ML/DM algorithms applied to bioinformatics: clustering, HMMs, supervised ML.
contents: Probability, Bayesian inference, hypothesis testing, linear models, PCA, clustering, HMMs, supervised ML, elastic net.
professorResearch: ML methods for biomedical data, drug discovery, disease progression modeling.
thesisPotential: Medium – If interested in biomedical AI.
strategicAdvice: Only if you're interested in biomedical AI applications. Not aligned with your core NLP/ML/robotics goals.

name: Seminar Biological Intelligence
whatYouLearn: Biological vs artificial agent capabilities: learning, planning, causal reasoning, language, spatial navigation.
contents: Biological skills, computational theories of cognition, implications for AI agent design.
professorResearch: Computational neuroscience, cognitive modeling of biological agents.
strategicAdvice: Interesting seminar option if you want broader AI perspective. No prerequisites – accessible.

name: Cryptography
whatYouLearn: Symmetric/asymmetric encryption, digital signatures, hash functions, protocols, number-theoretic foundations.
contents: Symmetric ciphers, RSA, elliptic curves, digital signatures, hash functions, zero-knowledge proofs.
professorResearch: Computer algebra, cryptography, algorithmic complexity.
hiwiPotential: Low – Not aligned with ML/NLP goals.
thesisPotential: Low – Unless interested in security research.
strategicAdvice: Popular filler for the Algorithmics track (9 ECTS). Good for breadth but not your core focus.

name: Lab Cognitive Modelling of Biological Agents
whatYouLearn: Cognitive modeling workflow: RL-based solutions, behavioral signatures, experiment design, data analysis.
contents: Cognitive modeling, RL theory applications, computational simulation, experiment design, behavioral data analysis.
professorResearch: Computational neuroscience, cognitive modeling, RL theory for biological agents.
strategicAdvice: Niche lab. Only take if cognitive neuroscience genuinely interests you. Not core to your ML/NLP trajectory.

name: Master Thesis
whatYouLearn: Independent research: solve a significant research problem under supervision, scientific writing, state-of-the-art.
contents: Topic from any area of CS. Must obtain 60 CP before starting. Register with exam office.
professorResearch: Depends on chosen supervisor.
strategicAdvice: Plan from Day 1. Build relationships with target supervisors through labs and seminars in Semesters 1-3.

name: Master Seminar
whatYouLearn: Present and defend thesis results to a scientific audience.
contents: Presentation of thesis topic, context, and results.
professorResearch: Depends on chosen supervisor.
strategicAdvice: Mandatory companion to thesis.
